user.name: Alma
user.bio: Privat investor & speculator | Mathematics | Self-made quant | Market researcher | Options and Futures trader

comment.body:
Thanks Alma.  I want to give Alma some major props but also share some sobering thoughts.  

For all of us, the ones on this stack and are fully confident in Alma's talent, we are extremely protected by her genius.  It's simply AMAZING.  All props and credit to Alma.  Alma - we can't thank you enough. 

On a flip side, there is a huge population of small traders, investors and average people who are likely in a huge panic right now, perhaps since Thursday.  Out of the kindness that all mankind deserves, if you interact with them, consider the empathy they need as the turmoil they might be going through can be individually catastrophic.  Yes, they are totally accountable for putting their money in their chosen investments, but it doesn't mean others need to make them feel worse.  

Thoughts like this come to mind knowing so many people haven't believed even the small bits that I have shared from Alma's call about this drop.  The markets will keep moving on, but human life is far more precious.

comment.body:
I’ve been through the dot-com bust, the great financial crisis, and COVID, but not trading like this.  

My concern is that this will not be a V-shaped event like COVID.  Instead, just like the dot-com bust exposed a large number of accounting scandals (and those accounting scandals did more damage to the markets than either the bust or 9/11), I worry that even a shallow recession will be the straw that broke the camel’s back in the commercial real estate market and cascade into banking/lending.
comment.raw_body:

comment.body:
The issue back then was larger than one company.  Back then, it was mostly revenue recognition.  So it wasn’t just pets.com and the like, but it extended out to MCI/Worldcom and Enron.  Because the accountants pushed the limits on what they were allowed to do.

So I don’t have an opinion on NVDA, but if they find problems there then you should expect it to pop up in other places.
comment.raw_body:
